questions.explain: Group elements are the grouplike elements: $\Delta(g)=g\otimes g$ and $\varepsilon(g)=1$. The primitive form $x\otimes 1+1\otimes x$ is what Lie algebra elements satisfy inside $U(\mathfrak g)$.
questions.q: In the group Hopf algebra $\mathbb{C}[G]$, what is the comultiplication on a basis element $g\in G$?

choices: It is a two-sided convolution inverse of the identity in $\mathrm{End}(H)$.
questions.explain: $S$ is defined by $m\circ(S\otimes\mathrm{id})\circ\Delta = m\circ(\mathrm{id}\otimes S)\circ\Delta = \eta\circ\varepsilon$ — a convolution inverse, hence unique. $S$ is an antihomomorphism, and $S^2=\mathrm{id}$ only in the cocommutative case.
questions.q: Which axiom forces the antipode $S$ to be unique once it exists?

choices: Because $\Delta(x)=x\otimes 1+1\otimes x$, $S(x)=-x$, $\varepsilon(x)=0$ extend uniquely to algebra (anti)homomorphisms.
Because $\mathfrak g$ is a vector space.
questions.explain: The primitive coproduct on Lie generators extends by the universal property of $U(\mathfrak g)$. Cocommutativity reflects that $\mathfrak g$ has no braiding — quantization breaks exactly that symmetry.
questions.q: Why is $U(\mathfrak g)$ a Hopf algebra?

questions.explain: Expanding $K=q^H = 1 + (\log q) H + \cdots$ gives $(K-K^{-1})/(q-q^{-1}) \to H$ in the limit, recovering the classical $\mathfrak{sl}_2$ relation.
questions.q: In $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, what does the relation $[E,F]=(K-K^{-1})/(q-q^{-1})$ become as $q\to 1$ with $K=q^H$?

questions.explain: Conjugation by the grouplike $K=q^H$ replaces the additive eigenvalue $2$ with the multiplicative $q^2$. Differentiating at $q=1$ recovers the classical bracket.
questions.q: Which relation is the quantum analogue of $[H,E]=2E$?

questions.explain: The Drinfeld–Jimbo coproduct twists primitivity by the Cartan element: $\Delta(E)=E\otimes 1+K\otimes E$ and $\Delta(F)=F\otimes K^{-1}+1\otimes F$. This is what kills cocommutativity and produces a braiding.
Reidemeister III / the third braid relation is exactly Yang–Baxter on three strands. The $R$-matrix gives a representation of the braid group $B_n$ on $V^{\otimes n}$.
questions.q: The Yang–Baxter equation $R_{12}R_{13}R_{23}=R_{23}R_{13}R_{12}$ corresponds geometrically to which braid identity?

questions.explain: Drinfeld's definition: a quasitriangular Hopf algebra carries an invertible $R$ intertwining $\Delta$ and the flipped $\Delta^{op}$. Cocommutative means $\Delta=\Delta^{op}$, so $R=1\otimes 1$ and the braiding is trivial.
questions.q: What property of $U_q(\mathfrak g)$ does the universal $R$-matrix witness?

choices: Module isomorphism $V\otimes W\to W\otimes V$ giving a braiding on $\mathrm{Rep}(U_q(\mathfrak g))$.
An algebra homomorphism $U_q(\mathfrak g)\to U_q(\mathfrak g)$.
questions.explain: $c_{V,W}=\tau\circ R_{V,W}$ is exactly the braiding making $\mathrm{Rep}(U_q(\mathfrak g))$ a braided monoidal category. Generic $q$ gives a non-symmetric braiding — the swap is no longer an involution.
questions.q: On a tensor product of two $U_q(\mathfrak g)$-modules, the operator $\tau\circ R$ (flip composed with $R$) is a/an…

choices: The non-negative integers $n$, with $\dim V_n = n+1$ — exactly as in the classical case.
questions.explain: At generic $q$ the representation theory is a deformation of the classical one — same labels, same dimensions, $K$ acts on the highest-weight vector by $q^n$. Roots of unity bring genuinely new phenomena (small quantum groups, tilting modules).
questions.q: For generic $q$, the irreducible type-1 finite-dimensional $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$-modules are parametrized by…

choices: A combinatorial graph (with coloured edges) computing tensor product decompositions.
The classical Lie algebra $\mathfrak g$ itself.
questions.explain: Crystals are coloured graphs where vertices are basis elements and edges record the $q\to 0$ shadow of $E_i,F_i$. The tensor product rule becomes a simple combinatorial recipe — Littelmann paths, Young tableaux, etc.
questions.q: What does Kashiwara's crystal basis recover when one specializes at $q=0$?

choices: It is bar-invariant ($\overline{q}=q^{-1}$) and has positive integer structure constants for tensor products and multiplication.
It is preserved by the comultiplication.
questions.explain: Bar-invariance plus an integral lattice condition pin the canonical basis uniquely. The positivity of structure constants is a deep theorem (and the seed of categorification — these integers count dimensions of $\mathrm{Hom}$ spaces in a categorical lift).
questions.q: Lusztig's canonical basis is distinguished from an arbitrary highest-weight basis by which property?

questions.explain: Each crossing becomes the braiding $c_{V,V}=\tau R$; the inverse crossing gets $c^{-1}$. Closing the braid and taking a quantum trace gives the knot invariant.
questions.q: Reshetikhin–Turaev assigns to a positive crossing of two strands labelled by $V$ which operator?

choices: The HOMFLY polynomial in two variables.
questions.explain: Jones falls out of $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ on $V_1$. HOMFLY comes from $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_n)$ for variable $n$, and Kauffman / two-variable Kauffman from $U_q(\mathfrak{so}_n)$ or $U_q(\mathfrak{sp}_{2n})$.
questions.q: Which knot polynomial arises from $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ acting on its 2-dimensional standard representation?

choices: Because $R$ satisfies Yang–Baxter (Reidemeister III) and the cap/cup data make the construction Reidemeister I and II invariant.
questions.explain: Yang–Baxter is exactly Reidemeister III. The Markov-style framing fix and the rigid duality of $U_q(\mathfrak g)$-mod handle Reidemeister II and (up to a framing factor) Reidemeister I.
questions.q: Why is the assignment 'crossing $\mapsto R$' well-defined as a knot invariant?

choices: The $R$-matrix action of a quantum group on tensor products of representations.
The Galois action on étale cohomology.
The Frobenius eigenvalues of an L-function.
questions.explain: KZ is a flat connection on configuration space valued in $\mathfrak g\otimes\mathfrak g$; transporting around braids gives exactly the $U_q(\mathfrak g)$ braiding, with $q=e^{i\pi\hbar}$ for the deformation parameter $\hbar$.
questions.q: The Drinfeld–Kohno theorem identifies the monodromy of the Knizhnik–Zamolodchikov equation with…

choices: Integrable lattice models and quantum spin chains (XXZ, six-vertex).
Classical Hamiltonian flows on cotangent bundles.
questions.explain: The $R$-matrix of $U_q(\widehat{\mathfrak{sl}_2})$ on a tensor power of the standard rep is the Boltzmann weight of the six-vertex model; commuting transfer matrices come from the universal $R$ in the second tensor slot.
questions.q: Affine quantum groups $U_q(\widehat{\mathfrak g})$ govern which family of physical models?

choices: The integers (dimensions, multiplicities) by graded vector spaces or chain complexes — promoting $U_q(\mathfrak g)$ to a 2-category.
Quantum groups by classical Lie algebras.
questions.explain: KLR (and Khovanov for $\mathfrak{sl}_2$) builds a graded 2-category whose Grothendieck group is $U_q(\mathfrak g)$; Khovanov homology categorifies the Jones polynomial, with Euler characteristic recovering it.
